name: Loop 14: Col de Bagargui from Mauléon – Basque Country passes
name_original: Circuit 14 : Col de Bagargui depuis Mauléon — Cols du Pays Basque
description:
This loop of about 80 kilometers is a magnificent summary of what the Basque Country can offer. From the capital of the espadrille to a thousand-year-old forest, the route fluctuates from valleys to passes for the greatest pleasure of the eyes.

The start starts from the village of Mauléon-Licharre. Considered the capital of the espadrille, it is a place faithful to Basque heritage and culture. From the village the road rises gradually to Aussurucq. This is where the climb towards the Auberge d’Ahusquy really begins. First of all it is a beautiful and dense forest which frames the road before the terrain is discovered at the foot of the Lomendi ridge. Once bypassed, it gives you the hostel in addition to a spectacular panorama. Its terrace allows you to savor the landscape for a moment before continuing.

The following portion continues to climb although it is more hilly than the previous one. The small descents allow you to cut the effort a little. It is by going around the Escaliers peak that you enter the Iraty forest. The largest beech forest in Europe finally gives you the Col de Bagargui, the highest point of the route.

At the pass, a grocery store and a restaurant allow you to get supplies before rolling out the beautiful descent that leads to Larrau. The view of the Pic d'Orhy, the highest point in the Basque Country at 2,017 metres, is remarkable. If you haven't made it to Iraty, the village of Larrau suggests a nice break. The Auberge de Logibar, a little later, is also an excellent place to stop.
It is here that you join the Gave de Larrau.

You stroll along the stream and the bends in the road. This gentle stroll continues when the waterway connects to the Season. From then on, you just have to follow it until you find the village of Mauléon-Licharre.
region_intro: Hiking around Mauléon-Licharre offers diverse natural landscapes within the French Basque Country, characterized by rolling green hills, deep river-carved valleys, and panoramic views of the Pyrenees mountains. The region features significant natural areas like the Arbailles forest, known for its karstic relief, and extensive beech forests. Dramatic natural gorges, including Gorges d'Holzarté and Gorges de Kakoetta, provide unique hiking experiences. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking experiences, from paths along rivers to ascents in the foothills of the Pyrenees. Best hiking trails around Mauléon-Licharre The most popular hiking route is Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Ordiarp, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete, offering expansive views of the Pyrenees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mauléon-Licharre loop from Mauléon-Licharre, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path winding through the local landscapes around the town. Local hikers also love the Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Chapelle Saint-Grégoire, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through the Pyrenees foothills, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Mauléon-Licharre is defined by Pyrenees mountain views, dense beech forests, and river-carved valleys. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate paths, and more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Mauléon-Licharre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Mauléon-Licharre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Mauléon-Licharre?
A: Mauléon-Licharre offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 35 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, including easy strolls, moderate paths, and more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy hiking trails around Mauléon-Licharre, with 9 routes specifically categorized as easy. These paths are generally less demanding and can be a great option for families or those new to hiking. For example, you can find gentle routes exploring the local landscapes around the town.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails around Mauléon-Licharre are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24647603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mauléon-Licharre loop from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 10.3 km journey through the local scenery.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Mauléon-Licharre?
A: The region boasts diverse natural landscapes, from rolling green hills and deep river-carved valleys to panoramic views of the Pyrenees. You can explore the enchanting &lt;strong&gt;Arbailles forest&lt;/strong&gt; with its karstic relief, or discover the stunning scenery of the &lt;strong&gt;Iraty Forest&lt;/strong&gt;, recognized as Europe's largest beech forest. The area also features unique natural phenomena like the resurgence of the Bidouze river.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer spectacular viewpoints of the Pyrenees?
A: Yes, many trails provide breathtaking views of the Pyrenees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10525175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Ordiarp&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering expansive vistas. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35285984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great View of the Pyrenees loop from Chapelle Saint-Grégoire&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through the foothills with impressive panoramas.

Q: What are some unique natural attractions or landmarks to explore on a hike?
A: Mauléon-Licharre is rich in unique attractions. You can hike to the spectacular &lt;strong&gt;Holzarte Footbridge&lt;/strong&gt;, a suspension bridge hanging 180 meters above a canyon, or explore the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Kakuetta Gorges&lt;/strong&gt; with its towering rock walls and a 20-meter waterfall. The region also features the mysterious &lt;strong&gt;Occabé Cromlechs&lt;/strong&gt;, ancient megalithic structures offering stunning views from the summit of Occabé.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While Mauléon-Licharre is well-connected, specific public transport options directly to remote trailheads can be limited. It's often recommended to check local bus schedules for connections to nearby villages or consider carpooling for easier access to starting points for many of the region's hikes.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Mauléon-Licharre?
A: The region is suitable for hiking throughout much of the year, with each season offering a different experience. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, especially in the beech forests. Summer is ideal for higher-altitude trails, while winter can offer unique snowshoeing opportunities, though some paths may be less accessible.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural landmarks accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area is steeped in history. Sections of the historic &lt;strong&gt;Compostela Trails&lt;/strong&gt; pass through the region, offering picturesque paths to sites like the Stele of Gibraltar and the open chapel of Soyarce, which provides panoramic views. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1560257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of L'Hôpital-Saint-Blaise&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, which is a short distance from some routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Mauléon-Licharre?
A: The hiking experiences around Mauléon-Licharre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic Pyrenees views to the tranquility of the ancient forests, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any hikes that pass by the Col d'Osquich?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Col d'Osquich&lt;/strong&gt; is a notable mountain pass in the Soule region, offering magnificent views. While not directly on a specific komoot route listed here, many local trails in the surrounding area provide access to or views of this scenic pass. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Osquich&lt;/a&gt; as a highlight to explore.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to a castle or other historic buildings?
A: Yes, you can explore routes that incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35286197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mauléon-Licharre – Château de Mauléon loop from Mauléon-Licharre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate hike that allows you to discover the local landscapes while passing by the historic &lt;strong&gt;Château de Mauléon&lt;/strong&gt;.
tips: Not far from the Inhrupa pass and on the road to the Bagargui pass, the hostel allows you to take a well-deserved break with a beautiful view of the valley.

Located in the heart of the Iraty Forest, the Bagargui Pass is reached via a beautiful 28-kilometer climb from Saint-Jean-le-Vieux. The climb averages 4%, with peaks reaching 11%. 
At the pass, you'll find the famous Iraty chalets and a grocery store for supplies.
Larrau is a charming Basque village nestled at the foot of the Pic d'Orhy in Haute-Soule.
Very pretty section along the Gave de Larrau. The road meanders along the curves of the river flowing in the bottom of the valley.

Authentic Basque village, Mauléon-Licharre is considered the capital of the espadrille. It is also that of the small province of the Basque Country, Soule. The heights of its old town are separated from the lower town by the course of the Season. We find in this part the castle of Andurain de Maytie.
